steps.title: Express an 8% increase algebraically
steps.content:
An increase of 8% adds 0.08 times the original amount to the original:
$$\text{2019 population} = \text{2018 population} + 0.08 \cdot \text{2018 population}$$
Factor the right side:
$$\text{2019 population} = (1 + 0.08) \cdot \text{2018 population} = 1.08 \cdot \text{2018 population}$$

steps.title: Identify the constant k
steps.content: The problem states that the 2019 population is $k$ times the 2018 population. From our factorization, this multiplier is $1.08$, so $k = 1.08$. That matches Choice C — $1.08$.
